steps.title: Match corresponding vertices
steps.content: The similarity statement says triangle $CAE$ is similar to triangle $CBD$. So $C$ corresponds to $C$, $A$ corresponds to $B$, and $E$ corresponds to $D$.

steps.title: Use the matching angle
steps.content: Corresponding angles in similar triangles are congruent. Therefore angle $CAE$ matches angle $CBD$. The side relationship changes lengths, not angle measures.

steps.title: Substitute the given measure
steps.content: The question gives angle $CBD$ as $57^\circ$. Since angle $CAE$ has the same measure, angle $CAE$ is also $57^\circ$. The answer is C.
